evidence.claim: Led team migration from keyword-search to embedding-based retrieval
evidence.source_text: Recently, I led the team that migrated our keyword-search-based product to embedding-based retrieval.

evidence.claim: Trained and shipped ranking models for discovery feed using XGBoost and LightGBM
evidence.source_text: Trained and shipped multiple ranking models for our product's discovery feed using XGBoost and LightGBM.

evidence.claim: Designed features across content metadata, user behavior signals, and item engagement history
evidence.source_text: Designed features across three families: content metadata, user behavior signals, and item engagement history.

evidence.claim: Owned offline-online correlation analysis for A/B test metrics
evidence.source_text: Owned the offline-online correlation analysis that determined which offline metrics actually predicted A/B test outcomes.

evidence.claim: Collaborated with PMs to define optimization targets
evidence.source_text: Worked closely with PMs to define the optimization target (click-through vs. dwell time vs. downstream conversion)

evidence.claim: Built content recommendation system serving 10M+ users
evidence.source_text: Built a content recommendation system serving 10M+ users

evidence.claim: Implemented item-item similarity via sentence-transformer embeddings for cold starts
evidence.source_text: The system uses item-item similarity (via sentence-transformer embeddings) for cold starts
Most of my time went into the feature pipeline (~200 features)

evidence.claim: Improved time spent per session by 14%
evidence.source_text: and time spent per session by 14%.

evidence.claim: Values practical experience over theoretical background
evidence.source_text: my main learning has come from shipping real systems and seeing what holds up under production load.

evidence.claim: Views retrieval problems as evaluation problems
evidence.source_text: I've learned that most retrieval problems are actually evaluation problems in disguise.
